levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to demonstrate movement skills.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: Demonstrates basic movement skills with significant support in familiar environments.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Applies movement skills independently in familiar environments but struggles with transfer to new situations.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Develops, applies and transfers movement skills effectively across different environments and situations independently.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Adapts and creates movement skills fluently across complex and unfamiliar environments.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner develops, applies and transfers movement skills effectively and independently, demonstrating full achievement of the learning target without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain balanced diet principles.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some nutrition concepts but explanations are inaccurate or incomplete.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Explains basic balanced diet principles but struggles to connect nutrition with health impacts.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Explains balanced diet principles and clearly describes how nutrition impacts physical health and well-being.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Explains nutrition principles and evaluates complex relationships between diet choices and health outcomes.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated at the learning target's level of demand without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to apply nutritional knowledge for food choices.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, applies basic nutritional knowledge to make simple food choices.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently applies nutritional knowledge to make food choices in familiar contexts.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently applies nutritional knowledge to make informed food choices that support health across different contexts.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers nutritional decision-making to novel situations and evaluates complex food choice implications.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot apply nutritional knowledge without first understanding balanced diet and nutrition principles.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently at the learning target's level of demand without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to plan or prepare meals.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some nutritious foods but produces incomplete meal plans.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently plans basic nutritious meals but preparation techniques lack consistency or accuracy.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently plans and prepares a variety of nutritious meals using appropriate cooking and preparation techniques consistently.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Adapts meal planning and preparation techniques to accommodate dietary restrictions or cultural preferences.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot plan nutritious meals without understanding balanced diet principles.
Making informed food choices typically precedes meal planning and preparation skills.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently' and 'consistently' indicating complete mastery of the learning target.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to analyze physical-emotional connections.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies basic connections between physical and emotional changes.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies connections but analysis lacks depth or context consideration.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently analyzes and evaluates how physical and emotional changes connect across different contexts.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers analysis to novel situations and integrates multiple interconnected physical-emotional patterns.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot analyze physical-emotional connections without understanding how experiences affect emotions.
Self-awareness of perceptions and feelings typically precedes analyzing their connections to physical changes.
Understanding emotional regulation strategies supports deeper analysis of physical-emotional connections.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor directly states the learner independently demonstrates the exact capability described in the learning target without hedging or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to modify behavior for health outcomes.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, attempts basic behavioral changes but inconsistently applies strategies.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently modifies some behaviors but struggles to collaborate effectively or sustain changes.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently modifies behavior and collaborates with others to achieve improved physical and emotional health outcomes.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers behavioral strategies across contexts and supports others in developing health-promoting behaviors.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot modify behavior for health without understanding physical-emotional connections.
Self-regulation strategies are foundational for sustained behavioral modification.
Recognizing health risks typically precedes modifying behavior to address them.
Communication skills support effective collaboration for health outcomes.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and frames Competent as successfully achieving the learning target without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify health risks.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some health risks but descriptions are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies obvious health risks but struggles to describe their effects on well-being.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently recognizes and describes various behaviors, conditions and situations that affect physical health and well-being.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Recognizes subtle health risks and describes complex interconnections between multiple health factors.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding nutrition principles helps recognize diet-related health risks.
General risk identification skills are essential for recognizing health-specific risks.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently at the learning target's level of demand without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to apply health risk management strategies.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, applies basic strategies but inconsistently or inappropriately for context.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently applies some appropriate strategies but misses key protective measures or contexts.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently applies appropriate strategies to manage health risks and protect self and others effectively.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Adapts and combines strategies flexibly across novel situations and advocates for others' protection.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot apply strategies to manage health risks without first recognizing what those risks are.
Risk identification and assessment skills typically support health risk management strategies.
General risk management strategies provide foundation for health-specific risk management approaches.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner independently applies appropriate strategies effectively, demonstrating complete achievement of the learning target without hedging language or dependency on higher levels.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to focus attention on internal experiences.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, notices some perceptions, thoughts or feelings but inconsistently.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently notices internal experiences but struggles to apply self-awareness consistently.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently focuses attention on perceptions, thoughts and feelings to develop and apply self-awareness effectively.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers mindful self-awareness skills to novel situations and integrates with broader reflection.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and effectively, standing alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify or apply emotional regulation strategies.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies basic emotional regulation strategies but applies them inconsistently or inaccurately.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies appropriate emotional regulation strategies but applies them inconsistently across different situations.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies and consistently applies appropriate emotional regulation strategies to manage emotions effectively across different situations.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers emotional regulation strategies to novel situations and adapts strategies based on contextual demands.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Self-awareness of thoughts and feelings is necessary before regulating emotions strategically.
Understanding emotional impact typically precedes learning to regulate those emotions.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently identifies and consistently applies' showing complete mastery of the learning target.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to communicate about feelings.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some feelings but struggles to express or advocate for communication benefits.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently expresses feelings but inconsistently advocates for communication as a mental health strategy.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently notices, expresses, and advocates for the benefits of communicating feelings as a mental health strategy.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers communication advocacy skills to support others and applies across diverse mental health contexts.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Self-awareness of feelings is required before one can notice and express them.
Basic communication skills are essential for expressing feelings effectively.
Understanding emotional impact typically precedes advocating for communication as mental health strategy.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently' performing all required actions without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify mental health support resources.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some people or groups but shows confusion about appropriateness.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies some appropriate support people or groups but lacks understanding of timing considerations.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies appropriate people, groups, and timing for seeking mental health support across different situations.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Evaluates and prioritizes multiple support options while considering complex situational factors and accessibility.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding the value of communicating feelings typically precedes knowing where to seek support.
Self-awareness helps recognize when mental health support might be needed.
Understanding emotional impact helps determine appropriate timing for seeking support.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner can independently demonstrate the capability without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to analyze past experiences.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies basic details from experiences but struggles to recognize patterns.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies some patterns or impacts from experiences but analysis lacks depth.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently analyzes past experiences to identify clear patterns, impacts, and meaningful lessons learned.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers analytical insights across diverse experiences to generate sophisticated understanding and predictions.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Self-awareness is essential to meaningfully analyze one's own past experiences.
Understanding emotional impact typically precedes deeper pattern analysis of experiences.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently analyzes' and 'clear patterns, impacts, and meaningful lessons learned' without any hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to connect past experiences with future preparation.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some past experiences but makes limited connections to future situations.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently connects some past experiences to future situations but predictions lack detail or accuracy.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently uses insights from past experiences to make realistic predictions and develop appropriate preparations for future challenges and opportunities.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers predictive insights across diverse contexts and develops comprehensive contingency plans for multiple scenarios.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot use insights from past experiences without first being able to analyze and reflect on them.
Goal-setting skills typically support the preparation aspect of anticipating future situations.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently uses insights' and 'realistic predictions' without any hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to understand emotional impact.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies basic emotions but struggles to connect experiences with impacts.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently recognizes emotional impacts in familiar situations but misses underlying reasons or connections.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently explains how and why specific experiences create emotional impacts on self and others across various situations.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Analyzes complex emotional patterns and predicts how varied experiences will impact different people.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Self-awareness of perceptions and feelings is necessary to understand emotional impact.
Noticing and expressing feelings typically develops before understanding their causes and impacts.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and can stand alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to gather or use evidence for decisions.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, collects some information but applies it inaccurately or incompletely.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently gathers relevant evidence but evaluates it superficially or applies it inconsistently.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently gathers, evaluates, and applies relevant evidence to make informed decisions across different contexts.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Synthesizes complex evidence sources and anticipates decision implications beyond immediate context.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Analyzing past experiences provides foundation for evaluating evidence quality and relevance.
Understanding decision impacts is essential for applying evidence meaningfully in decision-making.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently' performing all required actions without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain decision impacts or processes.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some decision effects but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Explains some decision impacts on self and others but misses timing considerations or collective processes.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Explains how decisions affect self and others over time and identifies when collective decision-making is appropriate.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Analyzes complex decision impacts across multiple timeframes and evaluates optimal decision-making approaches.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding emotional impact is necessary to explain how decisions affect people.
Understanding group dynamics helps identify when collective decision-making is appropriate.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor directly states the learner explains and identifies the required capabilities without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to evaluate factors or participate in decisions.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some factors but evaluates them inaccurately or incompletely.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies relevant factors but evaluates implications superficially or inconsistently across contexts.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently evaluates relevant factors and implications when making decisions both individually and as part of a group.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers decision-making evaluation skills to complex unfamiliar contexts and guides others' decision processes.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot evaluate factors and implications without ability to gather and evaluate relevant evidence.
Must understand decision impacts and processes before participating effectively in decision-making.
Group decision-making requires ability to communicate needs and respond to others appropriately.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently evaluates' matching the learning target's demand without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify hazards or assess risks.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies obvious hazards but struggles to evaluate likelihood or severity.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies common hazards and attempts basic risk evaluation with some gaps.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies potential hazards and accurately evaluates both likelihood and severity of associated risks.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Systematically identifies complex hazards and evaluates risks using multiple assessment criteria and contexts.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Must recognize health risks before evaluating their likelihood and severity.
Evidence-based decision making supports systematic risk evaluation processes.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner independently demonstrates the full capability without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to develop risk management strategies.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, suggests basic strategies but implementation is incomplete or inappropriate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently develops some strategies but implementation lacks consistency or effectiveness in managing risks.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently develops and implements appropriate strategies that effectively reduce, eliminate, or manage identified risks.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Adapts strategies across contexts and evaluates their effectiveness to optimize risk management outcomes.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot develop management strategies without first identifying and assessing the risks.
Evidence-based decision making supports selecting appropriate risk management strategies.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently develops and implements appropriate strategies that effectively' manage risks, meeting the learning target without hedging language.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain group rules or norms.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some group rules but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Explains basic group rules and norms but struggles to connect effects on individuals.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Explains how rules, norms, and behaviors operate within different groups and their effects on individuals.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Analyzes complex interactions between multiple group norms and predicts individual behavioral responses.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding relationship types is necessary to comprehend how groups form and function.
Understanding how groups shape identity helps explain why group norms have effects on individuals.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor directly states the learner can explain the required concepts without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to recognize or follow social norms.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some social rules but struggles to follow them consistently.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Recognizes social norms in familiar situations but inconsistently applies appropriate behavior independently.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently recognizes and follows the rules and norms of different groups and situations consistently.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Adapts behavior fluidly across diverse social contexts and helps others navigate social norms.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot navigate social norms without first understanding how they operate within groups.
Self-awareness helps recognize one's own behavior in relation to social expectations.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently' and 'consistently' without any hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain social influences on identity.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some groups but provides inaccurate or incomplete explanations.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently explains basic social influences but lacks depth in connecting values to identity.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently explains how values, attitudes, and identity are shaped by different groups and social influences.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Analyzes complex interactions between multiple social influences and their cumulative effects on identity.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding group dynamics is essential to explain how groups influence identity.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner can independently explain the concept without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to evaluate values or social influences.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some values but struggles to connect them to social influences.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies values and social influences but evaluates connections with significant gaps or inaccuracies.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently evaluates how values are shaped by social influences and recognises similarities and differences between people's values accurately.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers evaluation skills to analyze complex value systems across diverse cultural or historical contexts.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot evaluate how values are shaped without first understanding that social influences shape identity and values.
Self-awareness of own perceptions and thoughts supports recognition of one's own values before evaluating them.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and accurately, standing alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify relationship types or dynamics.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some relationship types but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies basic relationship types but explanations of change and complexity lack detail.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies different types of relationships and explains how relationships change over time and vary in complexity.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Analyzes relationship patterns across contexts and predicts how specific factors influence relationship development.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding emotional impact is essential for explaining how relationships change and affect people.
Understanding group dynamics provides foundation for comprehending relationship complexity and variation.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner independently demonstrates the full capability described in the learning target without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to communicate needs or respond to others.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, attempts basic communication but struggles with clarity or appropriate responses.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Communicates needs and feelings with some clarity but inconsistently responds appropriately to others.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently communicates needs and feelings clearly and responds appropriately to others' expressed needs and feelings.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Adapts communication style effectively across diverse contexts and facilitates others' communication.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Self-awareness is required to identify and communicate one's own needs and feelings.
Understanding feelings communication benefits typically precedes broader communication skills development.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently' and 'clearly' without any hedging language or implications of incompleteness.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify conflicts or apply resolution strategies.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, recognizes some conflicts but applies strategies inconsistently or inappropriately.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies conflicts and applies basic strategies but struggles with complex disagreements.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies conflicts and applies appropriate resolution strategies while seeking support when needed.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers conflict resolution skills to novel situations and helps others resolve disagreements.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Self-awareness is essential for recognizing one's role in conflicts.
Emotional regulation skills are typically needed before managing interpersonal conflicts.
Clear communication is fundamental to applying conflict resolution strategies.
Knowing when to seek support is usually established before applying it in conflicts.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner independently demonstrates all components of the learning target without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify relationship safety characteristics.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some basic safety features but responses are inappropriate or incomplete.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies obvious safety characteristics but struggles to respond appropriately when safety is threatened.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies characteristics of safe relationships and responds appropriately when safety is threatened in various contexts.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers safety recognition skills to complex situations and supports others in maintaining relationship safety.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Must understand relationship types before identifying safety characteristics within them.
Communication skills typically support expressing safety concerns and responding appropriately.
Risk identification skills provide foundation for recognizing relationship safety threats.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently identifies' and 'responds appropriately' without any hedging language or implications of incompleteness.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to understand or advocate for rights.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some basic rights but struggles to connect them to relationships.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently recognizes rights in relationships but advocacy efforts lack clarity or appropriate strategies.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently understands how rights impact relationships and effectively advocates for own and others' rights using appropriate strategies.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers rights advocacy skills across diverse contexts and helps others develop their advocacy capabilities.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding relationship types is essential before understanding how rights operate within them.
Communication skills are typically needed before effective advocacy can occur.
Recognizing safe relationships is fundamental to understanding rights within relationships.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently understands' and 'effectively advocates' without any hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.
